mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the effective federal funds rate reduces short-term borrowing costs and boosts liquidity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS decreases are not significantly higher than returns following months with FEDFUNDS increases (Mann-Whitney U p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, it signals labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following triggers where UNRATE > 6.5%, fewer than 55% of realized 60-day forward returns are negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a broad-based demand expansion that amplifies corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO increase month-over-month, the proportion of positive 20-day S&P 500 returns is not at least 5 percentage points above the unconditional positive rate over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is above 2%, month-over-month declines in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal disinflation that reduces corporate pricing power and nominal revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS > 2% regimes, months with declining CPIAUCSL fail to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal durable demand growth in interest-rate-sensitive sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month HOUST increases do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in both the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate a Goldilocks disinflationary easing environment that lowers real rates and expands valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with concurrent declines in both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL do not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of joint-signal instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls by more than 0.5% month-over-month, it signals a contraction in manufacturing output that drags down aggregate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO month-over-month declines exceeding 0.5% do not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5.5%, month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal an overheating consumer demand cycle that boosts near-term revenue surprises, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During UNRATE < 5.5% regimes, months with RSAFS increases do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.
